name: Paella Para Ella
recipeIngredient: 1 large Ripe tomato, peeled, quartered, seeded, and finely chopped
18 medium Raw shrimp, cleaned and deveined
1/2 lb Fillet of red snapper, cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour oil into a large skillet and heat. Brown chicken pieces; remove to the casserole, which should measure 14 inches in diameter.
2. Brown cubed pork and lamb chops in the skillet and remove to the casserole.
3. Add onion and green pepper to the drippings. Saute until onion is transparent.
4. Add garlic, bay leaf, and finely chopped tomato. Stir and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Combine toasted saffron with 2 cups of hot broth, salt, and hot sauce. Stir into the skillet; bring to a boil.
6. Pour over meat and chicken in the casserole. Bring to a boil again, lower heat, cover, and cook until meat is tender (30 minutes).
7. Add shrimp, scallops, oysters, cubed red snapper, scrubbed clams, lobster meat, stone crab claws, and squids (remove part of the ink and chop coarsely). Cook at moderate heat for 15 minutes, or until shellfish turn pink.
8. Remove stone crab claws for later use. If desired, remove half the shells of clams.
9. Ensure liquid in casserole measures about 3-1/2 cups; add more broth if necessary.
10. Stir in rice and mix well. When it starts to boil, add the remaining 1/2 cup wine. Cover and place in a preheated 325 degree F oven for 20 minutes.
11. Cook artichokes according to package directions, then drain and use 6-8 perfect ones.
12. Five minutes before removing the casserole from the oven, uncover and arrange artichokes around the edge.
13. Place asparagus spears between artichokes in a cartwheel fashion.
14. Arrange stone crab claws decoratively around the casserole.
15. Cut 1 pimiento into strips and place over the rice. Fill the remaining pimiento with peas and place in the center of the casserole, pressing down to avoid toppling over.
16. Add parsley bouquets for color throughout the dish.
17. Sprinkle all with dry sherry and return to oven uncovered for 5 minutes longer.
18. Remove from oven and let stand for at least 15 minutes before serving.
19. In many parts of Spain, a lemon wedge is served with each portion to squeeze over the paella.
